Tokenize documents and pack them into flat binary memmaps with a train and validation split.
Stage two. Documents are tokenized, wrapped with boundary tokens, concatenated into binary token arrays, and split into training and validation files for efficient random-offset sampling.

Packing converts the corpus into contiguous token arrays stored as memory-mapped files, enabling fast random-window batch sampling during pretraining. A small validation fraction is held out for loss tracking. Supervised pairs are rendered separately with a response-only loss mask.
